seo.title: 100 Laravel resources for developers
seo.description: A curated set of practical Laravel resources for engineers.

content.intro: This resource guide provides a technical roadmap for Laravel developers to navigate the modern ecosystem, focusing on the Livewire vs. Inertia decision, scaling infrastructure, and implementing advanced patterns like multi-tenancy and AI integration.

sections.heading: Frontend Interactivity: Livewire and Inertia.js Ecosystem

items.title: Livewire Volt
items.description: Implement single-file components in Livewire to reduce boilerplate. Use the functional API to define state and actions within a single Blade file, mirroring modern frontend framework ergonomics.

items.title: Inertia.js SSR Configuration
items.description: Set up Server-Side Rendering for Inertia applications using the @inertiajs/vue3 or @inertiajs/react packages to improve SEO and initial page load speed on content-heavy routes.

items.title: Laravel Precognition
items.description: Utilize Precognition to provide real-time validation for Inertia or Livewire forms by making 'probing' requests to the backend before the final submission.

items.title: Ziggy Route Integration
items.description: Expose Laravel named routes to your JavaScript frontend via the route() helper, ensuring your Inertia.js links stay synchronized with your web.php file.

items.title: Flux UI Components
items.description: Leverage the official Flux library for high-quality, accessible Livewire components to accelerate UI development without building primitives from scratch.

items.title: Alpine.js Global Stores
items.description: Manage shared client-side state in Livewire applications using Alpine.store() to avoid unnecessary server round-trips for UI-only state changes.

items.title: Inertia Persistent Layouts
items.description: Implement persistent layouts in Vue or React to maintain state (like audio players or sidebar scroll positions) across page navigations in Inertia apps.

items.title: Livewire Entangle
items.description: Synchronize Alpine.js properties with Livewire component properties using the @entangle directive for seamless two-way data binding between JS and PHP.

items.title: TallStackUI Library
items.description: Adopt this component library specifically designed for the TALL stack to implement complex elements like date pickers and modals with zero JS configuration.

items.title: Inertia Manual Visits
items.description: Use router.visit() for programmatic navigation and partial reloads, allowing you to refresh specific data properties without a full page reload.

items.title: Livewire Wire-Navigate
items.description: Enable SPA-like navigation in traditional Blade apps by adding the wire:navigate attribute to anchor tags, utilizing pjax-style content swapping.

items.title: Computed Properties in Livewire
items.description: Use the #[Computed] attribute to cache expensive data lookups within a single request cycle, preventing redundant database queries during re-renders.

items.title: Laravel Octane
items.description: Boost application performance by serving apps via Swoole or RoadRunner, keeping the application in memory to eliminate the PHP bootstrapping overhead.

items.title: Laravel Horizon
items.description: Monitor and manage Redis-backed queues with a dashboard. Use it to configure job wait times, throughput, and failure retries in a dedicated config file.

items.title: Laravel Reverb
items.description: Deploy the first-party, high-speed WebSocket server built for Laravel to handle real-time events without external dependencies like Pusher or Ably.

items.title: Laravel Pulse
items.description: Install this health-monitoring dashboard to identify slow routes, high-memory jobs, and bottlenecked database queries in real-time.

items.title: Redis Rate Limiting
items.description: Implement the RateLimiter facade with a Redis store to manage API consumption limits across horizontally scaled application servers.

items.title: Laravel Vapor Serverless
items.description: Deploy applications to AWS Lambda using Vapor to handle auto-scaling and manage serverless databases, queues, and caches via a single CLI.

items.title: Forge Site Isolation
items.description: Use Forge's User Isolation feature to run multiple client sites on one server while ensuring each process runs under a unique system user for security.

items.title: Query Caching with Cache::remember
items.description: Wrap expensive Eloquent queries in Cache::remember blocks with unique keys to reduce database load for frequently accessed, static data.

items.title: Database Index Optimization
items.description: Use Laravel migrations to add composite indexes on columns frequently used in WHERE and ORDER BY clauses to optimize query execution plans.

items.title: Laravel Envoy
items.description: Define SSH tasks in a Blade-like syntax to automate deployments, backups, and remote server management across multiple target machines.

items.title: Telescope Pruning
items.description: Configure the telescope:prune command in your scheduler to prevent the telescope_entries table from bloating your database in high-traffic environments.

items.title: Horizontal Scaling with Session Redis
items.description: Switch from file-based sessions to Redis in session.php to allow users to stay logged in while being load-balanced across multiple web servers.

items.title: Filament Admin Panels
items.description: Build TALL-stack based admin interfaces and customer dashboards rapidly using Filament's resource-based architecture and pre-built form/table builders.

items.title: Spatie Laravel Multitenancy
items.description: Implement multi-database or single-database multi-tenancy by using this package to automatically switch database connections based on the current tenant.

items.title: Laravel OpenAI Integration
items.description: Integrate LLMs using the OpenAI PHP client. Use Laravel's Service Container to bind the client and inject it into your services for AI-driven features.

items.title: Pest Architecture Testing
items.description: Use Pest's arch() expectations to enforce architectural rules, such as ensuring controllers don't call models directly or services remain isolated.

items.title: Saloon for API Integrations
items.description: Use Saloon to build object-oriented API integrations, replacing messy Guzzle or Http facade calls with reusable Request and Connector classes.

items.title: Laravel Pennant
items.description: Implement feature flags to rollout new functionality to specific users or segments without deploying new code, using the Pennant library.

items.title: Spatie Laravel Data
items.description: Replace generic arrays with strongly typed Data Objects that handle validation, transformation, and serialization between your frontend and backend.

items.title: Laravel Sanctum for SPAs
items.description: Configure cookie-based authentication for first-party SPAs to avoid the complexity of OAuth2 while maintaining secure, stateful sessions.

items.title: Laravel Scout with Meilisearch
items.description: Implement full-text search by syncing Eloquent models with Meilisearch via Laravel Scout, providing instant search results for SaaS applications.

items.title: Custom Eloquent Casts
items.description: Create custom cast classes to handle complex data types (like Value Objects or encrypted JSON) automatically when retrieving or saving model attributes.

items.title: Laravel Actions Pattern
items.description: Refactor complex business logic from Controllers and Models into single-responsibility Action classes that can be run as jobs, commands, or sync.

items.title: Spatie Media Library
items.description: Manage file uploads and associations with Eloquent models. Use it to handle image conversions, responsive images, and S3 storage automatically.

pro_tips: Always use 'Pest' for new projects; its 'arch' testing prevents architectural drift by enforcing that specific namespaces don't leak into others.
When using Livewire, keep your components small and use 'wire:key' on every element inside a loop to prevent DOM diffing issues during hydration.
Use 'Laravel Octane' with 'Swoole' only if your application is stateless; avoid using global variables or static properties that persist across requests.
For multi-tenant SaaS, prefer the 'subdomain' identification strategy combined with 'Spatie Laravel Multitenancy' for the cleanest URL structure and data isolation.
Leverage 'Laravel Pint' in your CI/CD pipeline to enforce a consistent code style (PSR-12 or Laravel preset) across your entire team automatically.
